---
title: "About this blog"
image: posts/2026-07-22-knowledge-has-no-limits/thumbnail.svg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rounded
  links:
    - icon: globe
      text: Blood Doctor
      href: https://dr-abdul-mannan.netlify.app
    - icon: quarto
      text: Quarto
      href: https://quarto.org
toc: false
---

I am **Dr Abdul Mannan**, a Consultant Haematologist and Director of the Bangor
Haemophilia Centre. This blog is two things at once: a personal record of learning
to publish with code, and a **living demonstration** of what
[Quarto](https://quarto.org) can build.

Every post here is a genuine, rendered example — not a screenshot. The survival
curves are computed in R, the pharmacokinetic models in Python, the interactive
sliders in your own browser, and the manuscripts, slides, book, EPUB and dashboard
are all built from the same plain-text sources you can read in each post.

## Why I built it

For most of my career, turning an idea into a finished scientific product meant
depending on a publisher, a statistician or a designer — and on costly proprietary
software. Quarto, with R and Python, removed many of those barriers. The analysis,
the narrative, the references, the figures and the final publication now live in
one reproducible workflow that I control.

Tools do not replace sound statistical judgement, scientific review or good
design. But they remove the friction between an idea and its final form.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
> Do not be embarrassed by your first attempt. Every polished skill begins with an
> imperfect beginning. Keep learning. Keep building. Keep creating.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Citation integrity
Bibliographic references in these demonstration posts illustrate the *mechanism*.
Before any real publication, every citation is re-verified live against PubMed and
Crossref under my Citation Integrity Protocol.
:::
